Comment: Flying wing on a RealFlight elevon model (conventional RealFlight flying-wing channel order: rf0=left elevon, rf1=right elevon, rf2=throttle). The "px4" values are HIL_ACTUATOR_CONTROLS.controls[] indices and MUST match PWM_MAIN_FUNC* in ROMFS/px4fmu_common/init.d-posix/airframes/1213_flightaxis_flyingwing (and HIL_ACT_FUNC* in init.d/airframes/1213_flightaxis_flyingwing.hil). DESIGN RULE: this table is a PURE IDENTITY PIPE for all twelve channels - rf N == px4 N, RealFlight channel N+1 is driven by PX4 output channel N+1, with no renumbering and no reversal anywhere. WHAT each channel carries is decided ENTIRELY by PWM_MAIN_FUNC<N> / HIL_ACT_FUNC<N>, exactly as ArduPilot puts it in SERVOn_FUNCTION. If the wrong thing comes out on a RealFlight channel, change the FUNC parameter - do not re-scramble this table.
ScaleComment: The "scale" column is the ONE thing here that is not free configuration, because it is a signal-domain conversion rather than a preference. PWMSim emits non-reversible Motor functions as [0,1] and EVERYTHING else as [-1,1] (PWMSim.cpp:77-88), while RealFlight wants [0,1] on every channel. So "unipolar" (pass through) is correct if and only if you assigned a Motor to that PWM_MAIN_FUNC<N>, and "bipolar" ((v+1)/2) is correct for servos, control surfaces and peripherals. Get it wrong and the failure is quiet: a motor read as bipolar idles at half throttle and never stops, and a surface read as unipolar loses its entire negative half and only ever deflects one way. RULE: change "scale" on a row whenever you change that channel's FUNC between a Motor and anything else. The values below match the FUNC assignments the shipped airframe sets.
ReverseComment: There is deliberately no "reverse": true anywhere in this file. Direction is not a property of the pipe, it belongs to whoever configures the aircraft, and there are two places to set it - both outside this file. (1) PX4 side: the PWM_MAIN_REV parameter is a bitmask over output channels, bit N-1 for channel N, and it IS honoured in SITL - it is applied in MixingOutput::output_limit_calc_single (mixer_module.cpp:531) which runs upstream of PWMSim, so it reaches RealFlight. Reverse channel 2 with 'param set PWM_MAIN_REV 2'; channel 9 is bit 8, i.e. 256. (Note PWM_MAIN_MIN/MAX are a different story - PWMSim overwrites them with setAllMinValues/setAllMaxValues, so per-channel endpoint trimming does NOT work in SITL.) (2) RealFlight side: each servo has its own direction in the model's servo setup. SYMPTOM of a wrong direction: the surface moves the correct amount but the wrong way, so the aircraft diverges instead of correcting - a reversed elevon pitches down when PX4 commands nose-up.
ElevonComment: A flying wing has TWO elevons and no separate elevator or rudder. rf0 is the LEFT elevon (CA_SV_CS0, type 5 -> controls[0]) and rf1 is the RIGHT elevon (CA_SV_CS1, type 6 -> controls[1]); PX4's allocator mixes pitch and roll onto both. The two must move TOGETHER for pitch and in OPPOSITION for roll - if a roll command moves them the same way, or a pitch command moves them opposite, one of them is reversed: fix it with PWM_MAIN_REV (bit 0 = value 1 for ch1, bit 1 = value 2 for ch2) or in the RealFlight model's servo setup, not here. There is no rudder row: unlike plane.json this model leaves ch4 and up unassigned.
AuxComment: rf3-rf11 (RF ch4-ch12) are mapped but carry no function in the shipped airframe: PWM_MAIN_FUNC4 and up are unset, so PX4 emits 0.0 on those outputs and the bipolar conversion turns that into a steady 0.5 - neutral, and byte-identical to the UnmappedDefault these channels used to fall back on. They are listed anyway so that using one takes a parameter change and nothing else: set PWM_MAIN_FUNC<N> to Flaps, Landing_Gear, a Servo, a Peripheral via Actuator Set, or anything else PX4 offers, and it appears on RealFlight channel N with no edit to this file. Remember the scale rule above if what you assign is a Motor.
RfModel: informational only - aircraft is selected inside RealFlight

Channels.comment: unassigned     <- controls[3]  set PWM_MAIN_FUNC4 to use
unassigned     <- controls[4]  set PWM_MAIN_FUNC5 to use
unassigned     <- controls[5]  set PWM_MAIN_FUNC6 to use
unassigned     <- controls[6]  set PWM_MAIN_FUNC7 to use
unassigned     <- controls[7]  set PWM_MAIN_FUNC8 to use
unassigned     <- controls[8]  set PWM_MAIN_FUNC9 to use
unassigned     <- controls[9]  set PWM_MAIN_FUNC10 to use
unassigned     <- controls[10] set PWM_MAIN_FUNC11 to use
unassigned     <- controls[11] set PWM_MAIN_FUNC12 to use
